description: Fujianese organized crime refers to crime syndicates composed of Fujianese people, similar to triad gangs. The term primarily covers Fujianese immigrant gangs in Hong Kong, but also includes native crime syndicates in Fujian province. They have been involved in attacks against Hong Kong pro-democracy protesters and collaborate with international criminal groups such as the Yakuza and American Mafia.

country_links.evidence_quote: monopolized underground casinos in Tokyo as its main source of income. Hong Kong media reports indicated that the Fuqing Gang collaborated with the Japanese Yakuza
country_links.context: The Fuqing Gang monopolized underground casinos in Tokyo and collaborated with Japanese Yakuza.

country_links.evidence_quote: collaborated with the Japanese Yakuza, the American Mafia, and Triads operating in mainland China in the operation of underground casinos and the trafficking of illegal drugs in East Asia.
country_links.context: The Fuqing Gang collaborated with the American Mafia in operating underground casinos and trafficking drugs.
